frameworks.name: internally-referenced neural population analysis
frameworks.one_line: reference neural activity to other neurons rather than external events to expose internal signals

frameworks.name: decision state decoding
frameworks.one_line: continuously decode an evolving decision variable from each region's population activity

frameworks.name: brain-state transition
frameworks.one_line: decision formation versus post-commitment as distinct coordinated brain states

core_question: How are internal decision-related signals coordinated across brain regions during perceptual decision-making?
key_assumptions: simultaneous multi-region recordings can expose signals not locked to external events
a continuously evolving decision variable can be decoded region by region
commit-time is a definable internal moment
manipulations: comparison of externally-referenced versus internally-referenced analyses
key_findings: decision-state decoders trained per region show large-magnitude co-fluctuations across regions
the moment of commitment is accompanied by a coordinated brain-wide state change
thousands of neurons across up to 20 regions were recorded simultaneously in rats performing a perceptual decision task
interpretations: internal signals, not just stimulus- or action-locked activity, profoundly shape decision-related dynamics
decision formation and post-commitment are distinguishable coordinated brain states
